front: Location: Louis Sullivan and Dankmar Adler designed its Transportation Building in deliberate opposition to the Beaux-Arts style of the other buildings

front: Architect: 'Make no little plans' — motto attributed to this man, which 'attacks designs that lack magic to stir men's blood'
Building: triangular skyscraper at the intersection of Fifth Avenue, Broadway, and East 22nd Street in New York City; originally named the Fuller Building
Building: a classical Greek column inspired its narrow triangular floor plan
Building: photographed in pictorialist compositions by Albert Stieglitz and Edward Steichen
Building: ground-floor 'cow-catcher' retail space apocryphally inspired Hopper's Nighthawks
Location: 1909 City Beautiful plan for Chicago, co-authored with Edward H. Bennett; created an unbroken stretch of parks along Lake Michigan through landfill
Location: resulted in the double-decked Wacker Drive to reduce traffic and established Burnham Park along Chicago's lakefront
Location: preserved a string of forests connecting several parks, called the 'Emerald Necklace'
Location: its namesake park contains a monument to Italian air marshal Italo Balbo, who led a 1933 propaganda flight from Rome to Chicago
Location: William Howard Taft hired this architect to redesign Manila and plan the summer capital Baguio in the Philippine pine forests
Location: William E. Parsons later implemented this City Beautiful plan for Manila, building the first Gabaldon schoolhouses
Architect: called an 'architectural imperialist' in Daniel Immerwahr's How to Hide an Empire for his colonial city planning
Architect: long partnership with John Wellborn Root; together co-designed the Monadnock Building in Chicago
Architect: after the Great Fire of Chicago, designed the Montauk Block with fire shutters and a fireproof iron skeleton
Architect: designed Washington D.C.'s Union Station; served on the McMillan Commission that redesigned the National Mall
Building: three-archway Chicago gatehouse with turrets and a terracotta bull's head at the center
Architect: designed the public library in Joliet, Illinois
